subject: a poet who won't sell out
lyrics:
[Verse]
Smoke curls slow above a downtown bar
He's got ink-stained cuffs and a borrowed guitar
They offered him gold for a softer line
Said sand off the edges, you'll do just fine

[Verse]
But the truth don't fold to fit a frame
And a watered word ain't worth the shame
He folded the contract into a crane
Let it ride out the door in the falling rain

[Chorus]
No, he won't sell out, won't trade the ache
For a brighter room and a bigger take
Let the spotlight find a sweeter name
He'd rather burn slow than die in the flame

[Verse]
The piano knows the things he meant
Every nickel of the rent unspent
He reads to twelve and an empty chair
And swears the dust can feel him there

[Bridge]
Maybe the gold was never the prize
Maybe it's the look in a stranger's eyes
When a line lands true and the room goes still
That's the only buyer he ever will

[Chorus]
No, he won't sell out, won't trade the ache
For a brighter room and a bigger take
Let the spotlight find a sweeter name
He'd rather burn slow than die in the flame

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
Gold on the cul-de-sac, sun going down
Boxes in the hallway, we leave tomorrow town
You painted your nails on my mama's back steps
We counted all the things we ain't done yet

[Chorus]
Hold on, hold on, just a little more light
The whole world's waiting but it's still tonight
Don't let the morning come too soon
Keep me under this falling-apart moon

[Verse]
We drove with the windows down, nowhere to be
Split a warm soda by the cherry tree
You said you'd write, I said I would too
Both of us knowing what time gon' do

[Bridge]
Everything changes, that's the deal we made
When we signed our names to growing up unafraid
But tonight is ours and the crickets agree
Stay in the in-between with me

[Chorus]
Hold on, hold on, just a little more light
The whole world's waiting but it's still tonight
Don't let the morning come too soon
Keep me under this falling-apart moon

subject: calling your mother after years of silence
lyrics:
[Verse]
The phone weighs heavy as a loaded gun
Been seven winters since the leaving was done
I dial the number that I never erased
And the dial tone hums like a hymn out of place

[Verse]
The crows on the wire know my every sin
The dust on the line where her voice comes in
She says my name like it never left
Like the silence between us was something I kept

[Chorus]
Mama, I'm calling from the edge of the dark
With a coward's hand and a hopeful heart
I buried our years in a shallow ground
Forgive me slow, but don't put the phone down

[Verse]
The clock on the mantel still keeps her time
The whiskey I left and the bitter and brine
She coughs and she laughs and she starts to cry
And the wire holds us both 'til the well runs dry

[Bridge]
All them prideful nights I let the embers go cold
Now her voice sounds older than the stories she told
There's a grave with my name if I wait too long
So I'm singing this sorry like an old church song

[Chorus]
Mama, I'm calling from the edge of the dark
With a coward's hand and a hopeful heart
I buried our years in a shallow ground
Forgive me slow, but don't put the phone down

subject: a getaway driver's last job
lyrics:
[Verse]
Engine screaming like a wounded god
Rubber on the rain and the asphalt scarred
They said one more run and the debt is clean
The meanest ride this town has ever seen

[Verse]
Three in the bank and the sirens wake
Doors slam hard for the devil's sake
I count the seconds like a hangman's rope
Foot to the floor, there ain't no slope

[Chorus]
Drive! Through the fire and the blinding light
One last run into the throat of night
No brakes, no breaks, no looking back
The last job ends where the road goes black

[Verse]
Bag in the seat and a bullet in the glass
Gotta outrun every ghost in the past
The map's gone red and the bridge is high
Tonight either freedom or I die

[Bridge]
They promised me out, they promised me clean
But promises rot like a gasoline dream
The tunnel ahead is a screaming mouth
I floor it and pray as the world goes south

[Chorus]
Drive! Through the fire and the blinding light
One last run into the throat of night
No brakes, no breaks, no looking back
The last job ends where the road goes black

subject: driving past your old apartment at night
lyrics:
[Verse]
Took the long way home on the interstate
Ended up downtown a little too late
There's the window where we used to fight
Somebody else got the kitchen light

[Chorus]
I'm just driving past, that's all it is
A red light memory, a faded kiss
I won't slow down, I won't pull in
Just let me look at where we've been

[Verse]
New curtains hanging where the cactus died
New name on the buzzer there on the side
I swear I can smell the coffee you made
In the cold blue dark of the colonnade

[Bridge]
Funny how a building holds a life
Like a pocket holds a folded knife
The street don't know we ever lived
The street's got nothing left to give

[Chorus]
I'm just driving past, that's all it is
A red light memory, a faded kiss
I won't slow down, I won't pull in
Just let me look at where we've been

[Verse]
The light turns green and I let it go
Four years gone in a single block
The radio plays an old slow song
And I'm already three streets gone

subject: two astronauts drifting apart
lyrics:
[Verse]
The tether snapped in the silver cold
Your hand reached out but the dark took hold
I sang your name to the empty black
But the stars don't give an echo back

[Chorus]
Drift, my love, drift, into the deep
Where the engines die and the comets sleep
I strum this grief on a broken string
For the gravity that won't bring you in

[Verse]
Your visor shrinks to a fading coal
A dying ember, a parting soul
I beat the rhythm on my own chest
A heartbeat lost in the cosmic west

[Bridge]
We trained for fire, we trained for flame
But no one trains for the silent same
The radio whispers and then it's gone
And still I clap to carry on

[Chorus]
Drift, my love, drift, into the deep
Where the engines die and the comets sleep
I strum this grief on a broken string
For the gravity that won't bring you in

subject: leaving an arranged future behind
lyrics:
[Verse]
Soft on the morning the harbor breathes
A ticket folded in my coat sleeves
They planned my wedding and my whole life through
But the tide is leaving and so am I too

[Chorus]
Goodbye to the future they drew in chalk
Goodbye to the quiet, obedient walk
I'll find my own warm and easy day
And let the gentle ocean carry me away

[Verse]
My mother's pearls in a velvet case
A polished name and a borrowed grace
I leave them folded on the kitchen chair
With a kiss and a note in the salt-sweet air

[Bridge]
Nobody's angry, nobody's wrong
It's just not the rhythm of my own song
So I hum a little, I sway a while
And I let the leaving feel like a smile

[Chorus]
Goodbye to the future they drew in chalk
Goodbye to the quiet, obedient walk
I'll find my own warm and easy day
And let the gentle ocean carry me away

subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
lyrics:
[Verse]
The band struck up an old slow reel
I didn't know how the night would feel
Then you held your hand out, plain and true
And I had no good reason not to

[Chorus]
So we danced, two strangers in the gold
Neither of us twenty, neither of us old
We don't know each other's names
But the fiddle don't care and the night's the same

[Verse]
You stepped on my shoe and we laughed it off
The cake was cut and the toasts were soft
Someone's cousin caught the falling flowers
And we spun away the borrowed hours

[Bridge]
Tomorrow we'll be back in separate towns
Different buses, different downs
But for one waltz under paper stars
The whole wide world was only ours

[Chorus]
So we danced, two strangers in the gold
Neither of us twenty, neither of us old
We don't know each other's names
But the fiddle don't care and the night's the same

subject: a barber who knows the whole town
lyrics:
[Verse]
Old Earl's got a chair by the window light
Been cutting this town since '69
He knows who's dying and who's in love
Knows the secrets the church don't speak of

[Chorus]
Sit down, son, let the clippers hum
Tell me where you've been and where you're from
I've shaved every jaw in this whole town
And I'll keep your stories when they put me down

[Verse]
The mayor sat here when he lost the race
The widow's boy with the tear-streaked face
The striped pole turning, the slow town clock
A whole life passing on the avenue block

[Verse]
He cut my daddy's hair and his daddy's too
Three generations through and through
The mirror behind holds a hundred years
Of weddings, funerals, and small-town fears

[Bridge]
When Earl retires they'll close the door
And nobody'll know this town no more
The scissors quiet, the chair gone cold
And a thousand stories left untold

[Chorus]
Sit down, son, let the clippers hum
Tell me where you've been and where you're from
I've shaved every jaw in this whole town
And I'll keep your stories when they put me down

subject: two strangers sharing one umbrella
lyrics:
[Verse]
Rain came down like it had a plan
I ducked the awning and there you ran
One little umbrella, two pairs of feet
Groovin' down a flooded city street

[Chorus]
We were sharing one umbrella in the pourin' down
Two strangers makin' rhythm through the soaking town
Shoulder to shoulder in the falling rain
Get a little closer, say it again

[Verse]
You laughed at my shoes all squishin' wet
I didn't even catch your name just yet
But the bassline of the gutter and the splash and the spray
Had us dancing seven blocks the long way

[Bridge]
The storm could pour all night for all I care
Long as you keep walking under there
A accidental meeting in a downpour groove
Baby look what one umbrella made us do

[Chorus]
We were sharing one umbrella in the pourin' down
Two strangers makin' rhythm through the soaking town
Shoulder to shoulder in the falling rain
Get a little closer, say it again

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
Doors slide shut and the lights flick white
Too many shoulders, too little light
My heart's a drum that won't slow down
Underneath the breathing town

[Chorus]
Breathe, just breathe, count the stops to four
Hold the cold steel of the sliding door
It's only a tunnel, it's only the dark
It's only my body sounding the alarm

[Verse]
The map on the wall starts to bend and swim
A stranger's elbow, the air grows thin
I grip my phone like a falling rope
And whisper a small electric hope

[Bridge]
Nobody sees the war in my chest
The quiet flood, the failing rest
But the next stop's mine and the doors will part
And I'll carry out my pounding heart

[Chorus]
Breathe, just breathe, count the stops to four
Hold the cold steel of the sliding door
It's only a tunnel, it's only the dark
It's only my body sounding the alarm

subject: a factory closing down
lyrics:
[Verse]
The whistle blew for the very last time
The iron gates closed on the assembly line
Forty years of my father's hands
Now rust and silence where the engine stands

[Chorus]
They're closing it down, the heart of the town
The smokestack falls and the gates come down
Clap for the workers, clap for the dead
For the bread we earned and the prayers we said

[Verse]
Maria swept the floor for thirty years
Now she sweeps her own salt tears
The foreman cried when he turned the key
And the dust danced slow in the factory

[Bridge]
Where will the morning men go now
With calloused hands and an empty vow
The machines go cold and the wages cease
And the only thing left is a bitter peace

[Chorus]
They're closing it down, the heart of the town
The smokestack falls and the gates come down
Clap for the workers, clap for the dead
For the bread we earned and the prayers we said

subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
lyrics:
[Verse]
Sunday morning, I slipped out the back
Left my hymnal on the old oak rack
The organ swelled and I didn't stay
Just tipped my hat and walked away

[Chorus]
I'm steppin' out light, I'm steppin' out clean
No big goodbye, no burning the scene
Just a quiet little exit, soft on the floor
A boy who don't believe like before

[Verse]
I loved them pews and the stained-glass blue
The coffee hour and the kindly crew
But the words stopped fitting in my chest
So I'm tippin' my way toward something else

[Bridge]
No thunder cracked, no lightning fell
No angry preacher wishing me well
Just a Sunday breeze and an open gate
And a swinging heart that couldn't wait

[Chorus]
I'm steppin' out light, I'm steppin' out clean
No big goodbye, no burning the scene
Just a quiet little exit, soft on the floor
A boy who don't believe like before

subject: two rivals forced to dance together
lyrics:
[Verse]
They called your name and they called my own
Two proud fires on a single stone
We hated each other across the floor
Now the guitar demands we settle the score

[Chorus]
Dance, you devil, match my heel
Let the whole world see the way we feel
Every hatred in a stamp and a turn
We're chained together while the candles burn

[Verse]
Your eyes are blades and mine are too
But my hand must rise to meet with you
The crowd holds breath at the bitter grace
Of two old rivals in a forced embrace

[Bridge]
And somewhere deep in the pounding beat
The war dissolves in the moving feet
I hate you still, but my God you're fire
And the dance pulls truth from the bitter mire

[Chorus]
Dance, you devil, match my heel
Let the whole world see the way we feel
Every hatred in a stamp and a turn
We're chained together while the candles burn

subject: teaching your daughter to drive
lyrics:
[Verse]
Key in the ignition, hands at ten and two
Slow down, baby girl, the road belongs to you
Mirror, signal, breathe out easy now
Daddy's right beside you, we gon' figure out how

[Chorus]
Drive easy, drive slow, let the engine hum low
The whole wide world is a place you can go
I taught you to walk and I taught you to run
Now I'm teaching you driving in the morning sun

[Verse]
You stalled it at the corner and you started to cry
I said laugh it off, sweetheart, give it one more try
The clutch caught smooth and you rolled on through
And I saw the little girl growing up too

[Bridge]
One day soon you'll drive away for good
Down some highway out the neighborhood
But every time you turn that wheel
Remember how your daddy made you feel

[Chorus]
Drive easy, drive slow, let the engine hum low
The whole wide world is a place you can go
I taught you to walk and I taught you to run
Now I'm teaching you driving in the morning sun
